title: Alansmia turrialbae
taxonomy: Alansmia turrialbae is classified within the family Polypodiaceae, which includes other species of ferns. This plant belongs to the genus Alansmia, a group of epiphytic ferns. The classification of Alansmia turrialbae further places it within the order Polypodiales, a large group of ferns. The class Polypodiopsida encompasses Alansmia turrialbae and other related ferns. This classification is based on the plant's morphological characteristics and molecular analysis.
uses: Alansmia turrialbae is a species of epiphytic fern native to Costa Rica. Unfortunately, there is limited information available on its specific medicinal uses. parts: Alansmia turrialbae is a species of plant in the family Polypodiaceae. intro: Alansmia turrialbae, also known as Cat's Claw Vine, is a climbing plant species native to Central and South America, belonging to the Polypodiaceae family of ferns. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may aid in the treatment of various health conditions, including arthritis, digestive issues, and immune system disorders. The bioactive constituents of Alansmia turrialbae include alkaloids, terpenoids, and other compounds, including isoorientin, isovitexin, and orientin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Alansmia turrialbae is commonly used to make herbal teas, capsules, and tinctures, which are available in various health food stores and online platforms.
